description: Ex-ante protocol for upgrading underpowered Level C pilots to Level B. Each blocked pilot gets a concrete densification plan with testable success criteria. The rule is explicit: we increase decidability without breaking the homogeneity of the observed regime.
core_principle: On n'augmente pas N pour augmenter N. On augmente la decidabilite sans casser l'homogeneite du regime observe. Any upgrade that changes the research question, the primary proxy, or the structural meaning of the time axis is forbidden.

upgrade_methodology.step_1: Document current dataset version (rows, temporal range, point density)
upgrade_methodology.step_2: Identify densification path (extended dataset if available)
upgrade_methodology.step_3: Verify invariants: research question unchanged, primary proxy unchanged, time axis homogeneous
upgrade_methodology.step_4: Test robustness with multiple segmentation points (3-5 candidates)
upgrade_methodology.step_5: Run full ORI-C pipeline on extended version
upgrade_methodology.step_6: Produce homogeneity checks (proxy distribution stability, regime continuity)
upgrade_methodology.step_7: Classify final status: conclusive / underpowered / incompatible

invariants.research_question_unchanged: The pilot must test the same hypothesis before and after upgrade
invariants.primary_proxy_unchanged: O, R, I, demand, S columns must map to the same physical/conceptual quantities
invariants.time_axis_homogeneous: The temporal resolution and ordering logic must remain consistent
invariants.no_post_hoc_selection: Segmentation candidates must be declared ex ante, not chosen to maximize ACCEPT

final_status_options.conclusive: Upgraded to Level B with decidable verdict (ACCEPT or REJECT)
final_status_options.underpowered: Signal present but insufficient data even after extension
final_status_options.incompatible: Framework does not apply — legitimate negative result

pilots.research_question: Does the SNe Ia distance-redshift relationship exhibit a cumulative symbolic threshold crossing consistent with dark energy transition?

upgrade_plan.dataset_source_upgrade: Pantheon+ augmented with Carnegie Supernova Project DR3 + CfA Supernova Survey (Hicken et al.) low-z coverage
upgrade_plan.primary_variable_unchanged: mu_obs (observed distance modulus) — same physical quantity
upgrade_plan.primary_proxy_unchanged: O = Hubble residual dispersion, R = LCDM consistency, I = cross-survey agreement, demand = dark energy deviation, S = cumulative SNe evidence
upgrade_plan.segment_structurally_missing: Pre-threshold (low-z, z < 0.05): sparse SN coverage means the regime before the proposed dark energy transition is undersampled
upgrade_plan.densification_strategy: Add low-z SNe from Carnegie + CfA surveys. Interpolate between observed redshift bins to fill pre-threshold gap. Do NOT add high-z SNe — this would change the post-threshold regime.
bias_risks: Low-z SNe from different surveys may have systematic calibration offsets
Interpolation between sparse bins may create artificial smoothness
Adding points specifically in the gap region could bias toward detection
stability_tests_required: Proxy distribution comparison (KS test) between original and densified O/R/I
Segmentation sensitivity: verdict must not flip when removing 20% of added points
Cross-survey consistency: densified points from Carnegie vs CfA must agree within 2-sigma
upgrade_plan.feasibility: high — data publicly available, needs z-ordered rebinning

pilots.diagnosis: Pre-threshold segment underpopulated. Pantheon+ has sufficient total SNe Ia but low-z coverage is sparse.

pilots.research_question: Does marine biodiversity exhibit a cumulative symbolic threshold crossing at major mass extinction boundaries?

current_status.dataset_source: Paleobiology Database (paleobiodb.org) — Marine Metazoan Genus Diversity
current_status.blocking_constraint: Insufficient post-threshold points; recovery sparsely sampled

upgrade_plan.dataset_source_upgrade: PBDB with refined temporal binning: stage-level replaced by 5-Myr bins in Cenozoic + Sepkoski compendium cross-validation
upgrade_plan.primary_proxy_unchanged: O = genus diversity, R = 1-extinction_rate, I = origination rate, demand = extinction rate, S = cumulative diversity
upgrade_plan.segment_structurally_missing: Post-threshold (Cenozoic recovery, 66-0 Ma): geological stages are longer in Cenozoic, so fewer data points cover the recovery phase after K-Pg extinction
upgrade_plan.densification_strategy: Reduce temporal bin width from stage-level (~10 Myr) to 5-Myr bins in Cenozoic. This adds real data points (not interpolation) because PBDB API supports custom bin resolution. Produce intermediate variants for sensitivity analysis.
what_is_allowed_to_change: Temporal bin resolution in Cenozoic (stage -> 5 Myr)
bias_risks: Finer Cenozoic bins increase resolution asymmetrically (better coverage near present = Pull of the Recent)
5-Myr bins in Cenozoic vs stage-level in Paleozoic creates temporal heterogeneity
Sampling standardization (SQS/CR) may change diversity estimates vs raw counts
stability_tests_required: Compare raw vs sampling-standardized diversity curves
Subsample stability: remove 20% of Cenozoic bins, check verdict
data_sources: Paleobiology Database (paleobiodb.org) API with custom binning
Alroy et al. (2008) standardized diversity curves
upgrade_plan.feasibility: high — PBDB API allows custom temporal binning

pilots.diagnosis: Post-extinction recovery data is temporally sparse. PBDB coverage for Cenozoic marine diversity is lower resolution than Paleozoic.

pilots.research_question: Does the LLM capability trajectory exhibit a cumulative symbolic threshold crossing consistent with emergent ability onset?

current_status.dataset_source: Synthetic data calibrated to Chinchilla/GPT scaling laws (Hoffmann et al. 2022, Wei et al. 2022)

upgrade_plan.dataset_source_upgrade: Densified intra-family coherent series: extend with additional benchmark releases from SAME model families. NOT a soup of heterogeneous benchmarks.
upgrade_plan.primary_variable_unchanged: Benchmark scores on standardized tasks — same capability measure
upgrade_plan.primary_proxy_unchanged: O = benchmark breadth, R = robustness to hard tasks, I = inter-benchmark coherence, demand = parameter count (scaling pressure), S = cumulative emergent capabilities
upgrade_plan.segment_structurally_missing: Both segments equally short (~30 pts each). The series is too short overall, not asymmetrically sparse.
upgrade_plan.densification_strategy: Extend via intra-family interpolation on calibrated scaling curves. Build real_family_core.csv (core family only: GPT, Claude, Llama) and real_densified.csv (extended with consistent benchmark families). Explicitly exclude contaminated benchmarks (leaked test sets, fine-tuned models).
bias_risks: Heterogeneous benchmarks: MMLU saturation vs HumanEval sensitivity differ
Model family mixing: GPT vs Llama vs Claude have different scaling characteristics
Benchmark contamination: models may be trained on test sets (MMLU leakage)
Publication bias: only best models get published, creating artificial monotonicity
stability_tests_required: Intra-family consistency: GPT-only vs full-family verdict comparison
Benchmark panel sensitivity: MMLU-only vs multi-benchmark verdict stability
Remove 20% of interpolated points: verdict must not flip
Contamination check: exclude models with known benchmark leakage
intermediate_variants: processed/real_family_core.csv — GPT + Claude + Llama only (core families)
processed/real_densified.csv — extended with consistent benchmark families
contamination_exclusions: Models with known MMLU test set leakage
Fine-tuned models (instruction-tuned OK, task-specific fine-tuned excluded)
Unreproducible benchmark scores (no public model weights)
upgrade_plan.feasibility: medium — requires aggregating multiple benchmark sources with family coherence
upgrade_plan.timeline: 2-3 weeks for data collection and preprocessing

pilots.diagnosis: Series too short. LLM benchmark releases are sparse; temporal depth insufficient for 60 points per segment.

value_proposition: These three pilots are the best candidates for demonstrating that the ORI-C framework can distinguish: (1) a true indeterminate due to insufficient power, (2) a true reject, and (3) a true positive. This distinction is extremely valuable for framework credibility.
anti_gaming_clause: A pilot does NOT change level just because a CSV has more rows. It changes level because the upgrade report demonstrates: (a) proxy consistency maintained, (b) homogeneity checks passed, (c) decidability genuinely increased, (d) verdict is more stable not just more favorable.
